extraction.one_line_summary: WOUT VAN AERT won Stage 8 into Lausanne with a perfectly timed sprint in final 150m on uphill finish, beating Matthews and taking 4-second bonus on Pogacar.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma controlled peloton for Van Aert despite having yellow jersey, revealing dual sprint/GC objective hierarchy.
Wright survived solo longer than Cattaneo despite Cattaneo's initial strength, suggesting Wright's superior pacing on long breakaway efforts.
GC teams protected leaders through technical finale despite sprint stage classification, showing wind/crash nervousness influenced positioning tactics.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: won uphill sprint against Matthews after team paced entire finale, demonstrating Jumbo prioritizes his stages even with Vingegaard in GC hunt
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: launched first in final 150m but couldn't hold off Van Aert, suggesting his uphill sprint timing remains premature
rider_signals.WRIGHT Fred: positive: only breakaway survivor with 3.5km to go despite being caught, showed stronger diesel engine than U23 Giro winner Cattaneo
rider_signals.HOYDUNK Neilson: neutral: described as hardest working domestique controlling breakaway gap, signaling potential for future breakaway policing role
